title: Move catalog persistence and queries to canonical Medusa modules

gates.name: backend catalog integration
gates.command: npm --workspace apps/backend run test:integration -- catalog product-detail

verify: Seeded categories, products, variants, prices, inventory, sales-channel links, and publishable API key are Medusa records.
Catalog and product-detail routes resolve Medusa Query from the request scope and do not read eshop_local_catalog_* tables.
Responses preserve FT-001 and FT-002 contract behavior and expose stable Medusa variant IDs for later cart handoff.

verify.summary: Canonical Medusa workflow seed, Query graph catalog/product-detail integration, stable variant IDs, pricing, inventory availability, backend/Admin build, boundary scan, Memory Bank lint, and strict doctor passed.

evidence_required: Medusa workflow seed output with canonical record counts.
Integration output for catalog and product-detail mapping.
Proof that runtime query code does not reference parallel catalog tables.
constraints: Use supported Medusa modules, Query, workflows, and migrations.
Preserve existing storefront catalog and product-detail response contracts.
Keep search and filtering moderate and PostgreSQL-backed.
invariants: Medusa Product Variant ID is the sellable identity for later cart and inventory flows.
Do not retain parallel custom product/category/variant tables as canonical catalog truth.
purpose: Replace the parallel direct-SQL catalog with canonical Medusa commerce records and query boundaries.
success_outcome: Catalog and product detail use Medusa-owned identity, prices, inventory, categories, and sales-channel visibility while preserving buyer behavior.
anti_goals: Do not redesign the storefront UI.
Do not implement cart persistence or checkout.
Do not introduce an external search service.

stop_conditions: Canonical Medusa records cannot preserve required FT-001/FT-002 behavior.
Implementation requires a breaking storefront contract change.
Verification cannot prove stable Medusa variant identity.
